explanation: Welcome to the topic of for loops! A for loop is used in C++ to repeat a block of code a specific number of times. It consists of the initialization (e.g., int i = 0), the loop condition (e.g., i < 10), and the increment (e.g., i++)
Welcome to the ternary operator (?:)! This operator is a shorthand for a simple if-else statement. The syntax is: condition ? value_if_true : value_if_false;

Welcome to arrays! An array stores multiple values ​​of the same data type contiguously in memory. In C++, for example, you declare an array like this: int meinArray[5]; Access is achieved via an index that always starts at 0.
